sanskrit: अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च । तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन ॥
hindiTranslation: हे कुरुनन्दन  तमोगुणके बढ़नेपर अप्रकाश, अप्रवृत्ति, प्रमाद और मोह  ये वृत्तियाँ भी पैदा होती हैं ॥
englishTranslation: O descendant of Kuru, when tamas grows strong, darkness, inertia, negligence, and delusion arise.
essence: Darkness does not stay still; it breeds neglect and confusion.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: When tamas becomes stronger, a person loses clarity, becomes inactive, starts neglecting things, and falls into confusion.
simpleInsight: It feels easier to catch stagnation early than to wake up inside it.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is still teaching Arjuna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, where a whole life is being decided in a single moment. After describing the signs of rising sattva and rajas, he now shows what happens when tamas dominates: the mind dims, effort stops, carelessness appears, and confusion follows.
modernRelevance: You keep scrolling at 1:13 a.m., telling yourself you will decide tomorrow. By morning, the problem feels heavier, and even simple action feels distant.
translationLiteral: अप्रकाशः (absence of light) / अप्रवृत्तिः (absence of activity) / प्रमादः (negligence) / मोहः (delusion) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / तमसि (in tamas) / एतानि (these) / जायन्ते (are born) / विवृद्धे (when increased) / कुरुनन्दन (O descendant of Kuru)
insight: It teaches you to recognize inertia as a pattern that breeds confusion, not as harmless rest.

quoteVariants.text: When darkness grows, clarity fades. Action weakens, and confusion takes over.
More darkness, less motion, more confusion.
I can feel when inertia is becoming confusion.
Notice darkness before it rules you.
